Agreement End Date.description: Date when the agreement expires or was terminated, in ISO 8601 format.

Agreement Start Date.description: Date when the agreement becomes effective, in ISO 8601 format.

Collection Id.description: The unique identifier (qualified name or GUID) of the collection.

Confidence.description: A percent confidence in the proposed adding of the member.

Element Id.description: The unique identifier (qualified name or GUID) of the element being referenced.

Expression.description: An expression describing a membership, relationship or classification.

Label.description: A label used to identify or categorise a relationship link.

Notes.description: Notes and observations about the element.

Resource Description.description: Description of the resource or how it is being used. From the ResourceList relationship (0019).

Resource Id.description: The unique identifier (qualified name or GUID) of the supporting resource in a ResourceList relationship.

Resource Properties.description: Additional properties needed to use the resource. From the ResourceList relationship (0019).

Resource Use.description: Describes how the resource is being used. From the ResourceList relationship (0019).

Start Date.description: Date at which the license or certification takes effect.

Steward.description: The identifier of the steward responsible for the element.

Steward Property Name.description: Property name used to identify the type of the steward.

Steward Type Name.description: The type name of the steward element.

Summary.description: A short summary of the element's meaning or purpose.

Usage.description: The usage guidance for this element — how it is intended to be used in context.

User Defined Status.description: A user-defined status value. Only valid when the primary status is set to OTHER.

Watch Resource.description: Whether the parent entity should receive notifications about changes to this resource. From the ResourceList relationship (0019).

Absolute Uncertainty.description: The absolute uncertainty in the data values — expressed as a long integer.

Aliases.description: Alternative names for the data field.

Allow Duplicate Values.description: Are duplicate values allowed within the data class?

Average Value.description: Typical or average value for the data class.

Containing Data Class.description: Data classes that this data class is a component of.

Data Class.description: The data class that values of this data field conform to.

Data Patterns.description: The prescribed format of a data field, e.g. credit card numbers. Often expressed as a regular expression.

Data Type.description: The data type of the field or class.

Default Value.description: A default value to assign to this field or class when no value is provided.

Grain Assigned Fields.description: Data fields (or data lens) assigned to this data grain via the DataGrainAssignment relationship.

Grain Statement.description: Defines the granularity of the data in terms of a single row/object

Granularity Basis.description: The basis on which the granularity is defined, e.g. time period, geographic region, customer segment. Stored as a string (UML type: string).
Granularity Basis.Journal Entry: The basis on which the granularity is defined, e.g. time period, geographic region, customer segment. Stored as a string (UML type: string).

In Data Dictionary.description: The data dictionaries this element is a member of.

In Data Field.description: Parent data field(s) that contain this data field, establishing a nested or composite structure via the NestedDataField relationship.

In Data Specification.description: The data specifications this structure is a member of.

In Data Structure.description: The data structure this field is a member of. If display name is not unique, use qualified name.

In Data Value Specification.attr_labels: In Data Class; In Data Grain
In Data Value Specification.description: The Data Value Specfiications (or Data Classes, or Data Grains) this Data Value Spec is part of.

Interval.description: The interval between data values in this grain — expressed as a long integer.

Is Case Sensitive.description: Are the values in the data class case sensitive?

Is Deprecated.description: Indicates whether this element is deprecated and should no longer be used.

Is Nullable.description: Can values of this field or class be absent (null)?

Length.description: The maximum length of a value for a field.

Less Granular Data Grain.description: A coarser-grained data grain that this data grain is a finer version of, via the DataGrainDefinition relationship (moreGranular end).

Link Relationship Type Name.description: The type name of the association between the two data fields, e.g. ForeignKey, DerivedFrom.

Linked Data Field 1.description: The first data field in a LinkedDataField relationship.

Linked Data Field 2.description: The second data field in a LinkedDataField relationship.

Match Property Names.description: Names of the properties that are set for data class matching.

Match Threshold.description: Percentage of values that must match the data class specification to qualify.

Maximum Cardinality.description: The maximum cardinality for a data element.

Minimum Cardinality.description: The minimum cardinality for a data element.

Minimum Length.description: The minimum length of a value for a field.

Name Patterns.description: Alternative name patterns for the element — often expressed as regular expressions.
Name Patterns.Journal Entry: Alternative name patterns for the element — often expressed as regular expressions.

Namespace Path.description: Namespace that the data value specification is organized under.

Ordered Values.description: Are the values of this field members of an ordered list?

Position.description: Position of the data field in the data structure. If 0, position is irrelevant.

Precision.description: The precision of a numeric field, typically the number of decimal places.

Relative Uncertainty.description: The relative uncertainty in the data values, as a proportion — expressed as a long integer.

Sample Values.description: Representative sample values for the data class.

Sort Order.description: The sort order to apply to values of this field, e.g. ASCENDING, DESCENDING.

Specializes Data Value Specification.description: The DataValueSpecification (DataClass or DataGrain) that this element specializes, via the DataValueHierarchy relationship.

Specification.description: The rules language used to specify this data class.

Specification Details.description: Additional values for the specification — for example, substitution values to insert into the specification string. Expressed as a map of key-value pairs.

Units.description: An optional string indicating the units of the field value, e.g. km, USD, seconds.

Value List.description: An explicit list of valid values for the data class.

Value Range From.description: The beginning of the valid value range. Expressed as a string to accommodate non-integer ranges.

Value Range To.description: The end of the valid value range. Expressed as a string to accommodate non-integer ranges.

Create Data Dictionary.description: Creates or updates a data dictionary — an organized collection of defined data fields serving as a knowledge base of preferred data definitions. Implemented as a Collection with the DataDictionary classification.

Create Data Specification.description: Creates or updates a data specification — a collection describing the data requirements for a project or initiative. Its members are typically data structures. Implemented as a Collection with the DataSpec classification.

Create Data Structure.description: Creates or updates a data structure — a collection of data fields that defines the structure for a data source.

Create Data Field.description: Creates or updates a data field — a named, typed element within a data structure. Supports nesting via In Data Field and data class assignment.

Create Data Class.description: Creates or updates a data class — a description of the values that may be stored in data fields. Supports composition via Containing Data Class and specialization via Specializes Data Class. Can be used to configure quality validators and data field classifiers.

Create Data Value Specification.description: Creates or updates a data value classification which represents the characteristics of a data value. DataClass and DataGrain are subtypes.

Create Data Grain.description: Creates or updates a data grain — a defined granularity of data that can be associated with a data field or data lens.

Link Data Field.description: Links or unlinks two data fields via the LinkedDataField relationship, with an optional relationship type name to describe the nature of the association (e.g. ForeignKey, DerivedFrom).
